footer.tagline: Open to junior frontend roles with an Angular focus.

about.title: About me
about.p1: After many years in technical, industrial, and organizational roles, I deliberately moved into software development. Since November 2023, my focus has been modern frontend development with Angular, TypeScript, clean component structure, and clear UI flows.
about.p2: I work analytically, calmly, and with clear intent. Instead of fast short-term fixes, I prefer solutions that are understandable, cleanly implemented, and maintainable for teams over time.

3.text: Junior Frontend Developer — ready to take responsibility in a team and develop features cleanly.

about.lead: I did not come through a classic developer career path. Today that is one of my strongest advantages: I bring technical precision, discipline, and systems thinking into frontend development.

bullets.location: Based in Aachen — open to hybrid or remote work.

structure.label: Structure
structure.text: I work with clear components, traceable decisions and a clean UI architecture.

ownership.label: Ownership
ownership.text: I finish tasks properly, remove technical rough edges and prefer clean delivery over rushed output.

communication.label: Collaboration
communication.text: Calm communication, reliable coordination and visible progress are my default in a team.

signature.label: What you get
signature.text: Not a generic career switch, but a developer profile with technical maturity, discipline, and a real quality mindset for frontend work.

analyse.label: Step 01
analyse.value: Read requirements carefully and break them into clear UI logic

build.label: Step 02
build.value: Build components in a structured, understandable way

polish.label: Step 03
polish.value: Polish rough edges, test properly and keep maintainability in mind

academyImpact.title: A turning point with impact
academyImpact.p1: In 2023, I had to leave my job as a CNC machinist for health reasons — and I consistently made the transition into software development.
academyImpact.p2: At Developer Akademie, I worked on hands-on projects and strengthened my workflow from planning and implementation to testing, documentation, and presentation.
academyImpact.p3: For me, that proves one thing: I can ramp up quickly, deliver, and present technical results professionally.
academyImpact.p4: I am very grateful to Developer Akademie for that.

contact.title: Say Hi!
contact.text: If you are looking for a structured junior frontend developer who builds Angular projects cleanly and delivers reliably, I would be glad to hear from you.

hint.title: Short & direct
hint.text: A few lines are enough — I’ll get back to you promptly.

form.title: Send a message

email.label: Your e-mail
email.err: Please enter a valid e-mail address.

privacy.after: and agree to the processing of my data for being contacted back.

form.note: The message will be sent via your default email client.
form.lead: A few lines about the role, team or project are enough — I will reply directly and clearly.
form.success: Your email app was opened. If nothing happened, please use the direct email link instead.
form.error: Opening the mail app did not work. Please use the direct email link instead.

contact.lead: Want to discuss a new project?
contact.subtitle: Want to discuss a new project?
contact.copy: Write to me if you are looking for a structured junior frontend developer who builds Angular projects cleanly, communicates clearly, and delivers reliably.

response.label: Response
response.value: A short message is enough — I will get back to you promptly.

focus.label: Topics
focus.value: Angular frontends, UI implementation, junior roles, team fit.

model.label: Setup
model.value: On-site in Aachen, hybrid or remote.

items.0: A short message is enough — I reply promptly.
items.1: Email, LinkedIn, or the direct form are all fine.
items.2: Best suited for junior frontend roles, Angular teams, and structured UI projects.

contact.footnote: Happy to receive a short role description, project context, or preferred start date.

content.title: Liability for content
content.p: As a service provider, I am responsible for my own content on these pages under the applicable general laws. Although the content is created with care, I do not guarantee completeness, timeliness or accuracy in every case.

links.title: Liability for links
links.p: This website contains links to external third-party websites. Their operators are solely responsible for their content. At the time of linking, no legal violations were apparent.

general.title: General information
general.p: Using this website is usually possible without providing personal data. Personal data is only processed when you actively provide it to me, for example by contacting me via e-mail.

logs.title: Server log files
logs.p: When you access this website, the hosting provider may automatically collect technically necessary data in server log files. This may include browser type, operating system, referrer URL, time of the request and IP address.

contact.title: Contact requests
contact.p: If you contact me by e-mail, your information will only be used to process your request. It will not be shared with third parties without your explicit consent.

external.title: External links
external.p: For linked external services such as GitHub or LinkedIn, the privacy policies of the respective providers apply.

0.label: Data processing
0.value: Only when you actively get in touch

references.title: References
references.subtitle: Instead of invented quotes, this section shows transparently how I work: structured, reliable, and calm under complexity.

0.title: Precision
0.text: I prefer clean execution over noise. Code, structure and handoff should stay understandable and reproducible.

1.title: Ownership
1.text: New topics do not intimidate me. I learn systematically and take responsibility for stable, understandable results.

2.title: Learning curve
2.text: My transition into software development shows how quickly I can absorb complex topics and turn them into concrete projects.

references.principlesLabel: What you can expect in collaboration

principles.0: Clear communication instead of unnecessary friction.
principles.2: Calm, reliable execution even under time pressure.

references.workflowLabel: What I stand for in projects

closing.title: No invented testimonials — but a clear working profile.
closing.text: I prefer showing how I think, structure, and deliver in a transparent way. That is also how my projects are built.
